title: New Beginnings

jokes.text: Leslie insisting on interviewing for her own job despite Ron's protests
jokes.setup: Leslie wants to interview for a job that's already hers
jokes.punchline: Her insistence on following protocol even when it's absurd
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - her bureaucratic obsession even when it hurts her own interests

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I know this job is already mine, but I want to do the interview anyway. It's protocol.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Leslie, you're the director. You don't need to interview yourself.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ron, rules exist for a reason. What if I didn't follow procedure? What kind of government would we have?

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: A functional one.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I'm going to need you to schedule that interview. And I'll need three copies of my resume, notarized.

jokes.text: Ron, come on, it's me. Give me special treatment.
jokes.setup: Leslie's entire premise was demanding equal treatment
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandoning her principles when they don't work in her favor
jokes.explanation: Perfect button on the cold open - Leslie's complete 180 from her opening stance

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: My entire premise was demanding equal treatment.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: But you know what? Forget that. I'm just going to abandon my principles when they don't work in my favor.

jokes.text: I also walked you down the aisle at your wedding
jokes.setup: Ron listing his qualifications to know Leslie
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of this incredibly personal moment
jokes.explanation: Perfectly delivered character moment showing Ron's dry affection through bureaucratic frustration

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I've known Leslie for years. I've watched her grow as a person and as a public servant. I've seen her at her best and at her worst. I was there when she got her first job at the Parks Department. I've watched her navigate complex political situations. I've seen her fight for what she believes in, sometimes against all odds.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: And I was there the night she cried in my office because she thought she'd never find love.

jokes.text: Give it all back to the taxpayers
jokes.setup: Leslie asks about budget priorities
jokes.punchline: Ron's libertarian answer that directly contradicts the department's purpose
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron philosophy delivered with perfect deadpan timing

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ron, what are your budget priorities?

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I'd like to cut the Parks Department budget by 90 percent. Eliminate unnecessary positions. And fire everyone.

jokes.text: Spirit dogs chart and the committee ruling on everyone's dog type
jokes.setup: A chart showing everyone's spirit animals
jokes.punchline: The bureaucratic seriousness of a spirit dog committee with no appeals process
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist comedy combining workplace bureaucracy with silly personality quizzes

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: A spirit animal is not something to be assigned lightly. This is a bureaucratic process with no appeals.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie Knope
dialogue.line: But Ron, don't you think people should have a say in their own spirit animal?

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: No. The committee has spoken. There is no appeals process.

jokes.text: You guys think I'm a border collie? So obviously a cocker spaniel
jokes.setup: Leslie seeing her spirit dog assignment
jokes.punchline: Her indignation at being mischaracterized, then immediate correction
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Leslie's need to control her image even in silly contexts

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: My spirit animal is a dog? A DOG? I'm obviously a bald eagle or a wolf or some kind of majestic tiger.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Wait, actually, a dog is perfect. Dogs are loyal, hardworking, and they never give up. That's exactly who I am.

jokes.text: Classic border collie. Has to control everything
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to redirect the meeting to work
jokes.punchline: Someone immediately using the spirit dog framework to analyze her behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that turns the silly game into actual character analysis

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Okay, can we please get back to the actual agenda? We have a lot of work to do.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, I think what you're doing right now is classic Anxious Squirrel behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: What? No, I'm not—

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Yeah, and you're clearly in Defensive Peacock mode.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Everyone knows Leslie's spirit animal is Ambitious Beaver. She can't help it.

jokes.text: Can I get a what-what? What? What? Thank you
jokes.setup: Tom asking for celebration of his accomplishment
jokes.punchline: The awkward literal response to his slang request
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe moment playing on miscommunication and Tom's enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Guys, we crushed it. Can you please acknowledge my victory by giving me some dap?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Oh, sure.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Here's the contact information for a dap contractor in the area.

jokes.text: Champagne app that pops whenever I say something awesome
jokes.setup: Tom describing a new app
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous narcissism of an app that celebrates your own statements
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character moment - tech obsession meets ego in absurd combination

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I created an app called 'Humble Brag.' It's like Twitter, but every post is automatically prefaced with 'I'm so blessed' or 'Living my best life.' It's the most narcissistic thing I've ever made, and I made it specifically so people could celebrate their own statements without any self-awareness whatsoever.

jokes.text: I can straighten this for you. There we go
jokes.setup: Leslie being told there's nothing she can do to help
jokes.punchline: Finding the most minimal possible way to be useful
jokes.explanation: Great character beat showing Leslie's need to contribute even when marginalized

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Well, I may not be able to solve all your problems, but I can be here for you in the most technically minimal way possible.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I will sit near you. Not next to you, because that would be too helpful. But in the general vicinity.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I'll even make eye contact if you really need it. Occasionally.

jokes.text: Don't sweat it, Lez-kno. Tommy's taking care of every last detail.
jokes.setup: Leslie offering to help with research
jokes.punchline: Tom's ridiculous nickname for Leslie combined with overconfident dismissal
jokes.explanation: Tom's tendency to create unnecessary nicknames and overestimate his preparedness

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I can help you with your research.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: No thanks, Lil' Sebastian's mom. I got this.

jokes.text: Larry's endless romantic speech followed by That literally went on forever
jokes.setup: Larry giving a heartfelt speech about love
jokes.punchline: Chris immediately undercutting the romantic moment with blunt honesty
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of romantic convention with perfect character-driven timing

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Love is the most beautiful thing in the world. It's what makes us human, what connects us to each other in ways that transcend logic and reason.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Actually, that's not true. Love is just a series of chemical reactions in your brain designed to make you procreate. Also, you have a stain on your shirt.

jokes.text: Cracker dust... I mean yes
jokes.setup: Romantic proposal moment
jokes.punchline: Ann choking on crackers during the proposal
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that undercuts romantic moment but feels somewhat forced

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Ann, you make me the happiest man alive. Will you marry me?

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: *chokes on crackers* Yes! Yes, I— *cough cough* —I will marry you!

jokes.text: No part of this is how I pictured it. I thought another cracker might push it through
jokes.setup: The botched proposal scene
jokes.punchline: Chris's matter-of-fact analysis of both romance and digestion
jokes.explanation: Great character moment mixing Chris's earnestness with absurd practicality

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: I have to say, that proposal was a lot like my digestive system. Unexpected, a little messy, and ultimately successful.

jokes.text: Unpasteurized cheese smuggling and What's that mean? I don't care
jokes.setup: Ben offering illegal cheese to employees
jokes.punchline: His nervous over-explanation followed by dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Ben's anxiety about being liked while breaking rules

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: So I may have acquired some cheese that's technically not legal to import into the country, but it's from this amazing fromagerie in France, and I thought you all deserved to try it.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I mean, it's not like I'm smuggling heroin or anything. It's just cheese. Delicious, creamy, probably fine cheese. The FDA is very conservative about these things anyway.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: You know what? Forget I said anything. It's cheese.

jokes.text: Are you in a cult? Can I join? Let's sacrifice Larry
jokes.setup: Ben's enthusiastic behavior
jokes.punchline: April's dark interpretation and immediate volunteer for sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - dark humor with casual cruelty toward Larry

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I'm so excited about this project! We're going to make such a positive impact on the community.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: So what you're saying is we're all going to die. I volunteer as tribute.

jokes.text: Everyone is the best. We're cutting vacation time. Let's eat vitamins. This is exhausting
jokes.setup: Ben trying to be both nice and authoritative
jokes.punchline: The rapid contradictions showing his internal conflict
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing Ben's impossible position through rapid contradictions

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Listen, I need to be firm here. You all need to follow protocol.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: But I really appreciate what you're trying to do, and I think you're all great people.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: So this is non-negotiable. No exceptions.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Though I completely understand why you might want to bend the rules, and honestly, I respect that.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: But you can't. I won't allow it.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: You're all wonderful and I love working with you.

jokes.text: I can't shower in five minutes
jokes.setup: New bathroom break time limits
jokes.punchline: Employee revealing they shower at work
jokes.explanation: Solid workplace humor revealing inappropriate employee behavior

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Did you know that under the new bathroom break time limits, some employees have started showering at work?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: What? That's insane! Where are they even showering?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: In the bathroom. Where else would they shower?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ben, there's no shower in our bathroom.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Not with that attitude.

jokes.text: That little dude's wound so tight, he's gonna pop
jokes.setup: Ben's contradictory management style
jokes.punchline: Employee's perceptive analysis of his mental state
jokes.explanation: Good observational comedy that sets up Ben's eventual breakdown

dialogue.speaker: Ben Wyatt
dialogue.line: I'm going to need everyone to work harder, but also take more time off. Be more creative, but follow the rules exactly. Think outside the box, but stay in your lane.

dialogue.speaker: Tom Haverford
dialogue.line: So what you're saying is you're having a mental breakdown?

dialogue.speaker: Ben Wyatt
dialogue.line: No, I'm being a dynamic leader.

dialogue.speaker: Tom Haverford
dialogue.line: Those are the same thing.

jokes.text: Bathroom breaks should be limited to five minutes? I can't shower in five minutes.
jokes.setup: New workplace rule about bathroom break duration
jokes.punchline: Employee's assumption that showers are a normal bathroom activity at work
jokes.explanation: Absurd workplace behavior casually revealed through policy complaint

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: New rule: bathroom breaks are limited to five minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Five minutes? What am I supposed to do, just pee and leave? What about washing my hands? What about a little primping in the mirror?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Those are all part of the five minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Well, what about a shower?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: A shower?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Yeah, you know, to freshen up.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Tom, we work in an office. Not a gym. Not a spa. An office.

jokes.text: Doesn't Ron look exactly like a blue flyer? There aren't that many different types of kangaroos.
jokes.setup: Tom's new spirit animal system with kangaroos
jokes.punchline: Admission that his elaborate system has very few options
jokes.explanation: Tom's typical half-baked idea execution - enthusiasm without substance

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I've developed a comprehensive spirit animal classification system based on kangaroos.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: You answer a few key questions about yourself, and boom—you get paired with your perfect kangaroo spirit animal.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: It's foolproof. Well, it would be if there were more than three kangaroos.

jokes.text: Spirit kangaroos replacing spirit dogs with everyone as blue flyers
jokes.setup: The previous spirit dogs system
jokes.punchline: Tom's lazier version where everyone gets the same classification
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Tom's shortcuts and the decline of office activities

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Yeah, I simplified the whole thing. Everyone's a Golden Retriever now.

jokes.text: Step one: Chill out a bit. Step two: Get up there and rip it
jokes.setup: Tom explaining his presentation system
jokes.punchline: His ultra-casual two-step plan versus Leslie's 80-step system
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast comedy showing opposite approaches to preparation

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I have a two-step plan: step one, come up with idea; step two, execute idea.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: That's not a plan, that's a description of thinking.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Well, it works for me.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I have 80 steps in my presentation system.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Of course you do.

jokes.text: Indiana blue ribbon panel on irrigation and drainage quote
jokes.setup: Leslie defending her system
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and boring organization that praised her
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Leslie's pride in bureaucratic recognition

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: It's not absurd! It's a perfectly logical system. Everything has its place, everything is color-coded, and cross-referenced with both alphabetical and chronological indices.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: It's absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Well, the Pawnee Historical Society for Meticulous Record-Keeping and Binder Organization just gave me an award.

jokes.text: When did they put a lamp here?
jokes.setup: Leslie asserting dominance over her domain
jokes.punchline: Immediately walking into furniture she doesn't recognize
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy undercutting Leslie's authority claims

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: This is my domain. I know every inch of this office.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I am in complete control—

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: OW! What is this?!

jokes.text: Are you trying to bribe us? No! Oh, God. I surrender
jokes.setup: Ben offering cheese to officers
jokes.punchline: Accidentally escalating to bribery charges and immediate surrender
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ben's panic and tendency toward worst-case scenarios

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Would you officers like some cheese?

dialogue.speaker: Police Officer
dialogue.line: Is this a bribe?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: What? No, it's just cheese.

dialogue.speaker: Police Officer
dialogue.line: It feels like a bribe.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I'm going to jail, aren't I?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I'm surrendering. I'm going with you.

jokes.text: Not a shotgun wedding, although yes she is pregnant and yes we just decided to get married today
jokes.setup: Clerk assuming shotgun wedding
jokes.punchline: Chris's technically correct but contradictory denial
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Chris's literal-mindedness creating unintentional irony

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Wait, are you getting married?

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: What? No! Well, yes. But not because anyone's pregnant.

jokes.text: All we did was punch jewelry store into our car GPS. I was getting gas. No, I don't remember it
jokes.setup: Asking about their romantic planning moment
jokes.punchline: The completely unromantic reality and Chris's blunt honesty
jokes.explanation: Good romantic comedy subversion showing their practical approach to love

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: So that romantic moment when you planned the whole thing out?

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Yeah, I didn't plan anything. I just showed up at your door with flowers I bought at a gas station and said 'We should probably get married.'

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Those were from a gas station?

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: The only flowers they had left. They were next to the beef jerky.

jokes.text: Hair strand binder security system with elk hair
jokes.setup: Leslie catching Tom in a lie about reading her binder
jokes.punchline: Her elaborate security system using specific animal hair
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist character comedy showing Leslie's paranoid preparation methods

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Tom, I know you didn't read my binder. I have a very specific security system. I replaced all the binding with hair from different animals. If you had actually read it, you'd be covered in hair from a llama, a chinchilla, and a very angry emu.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Leslie, that's insane.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Is it? Is it insane, Tom? Because you're not covered in animal hair.

jokes.text: You're pranked. Check this out. I took a picture
jokes.setup: Ben thinking he's in serious legal trouble
jokes.punchline: Revelation it was an elaborate prank with photo documentation
jokes.explanation: Functional prank resolution but fairly predictable structure

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: What's going on? Why are there police officers here?

dialogue.speaker: Police Officer
dialogue.line: We need to talk to you about some serious charges.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Charges? What charges? I didn't do anything!

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Actually, Ben, you did.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Jerry? What are you—

dialogue.speaker: Police Officer
dialogue.line: We have photo documentation of everything.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Dude, you should see your face right now.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Gotcha! This was all a prank! Look at these photos!

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: You guys... you got me. That was elaborate.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: We've been planning this for weeks.

jokes.text: Screw bisque
jokes.setup: Stu talking about retirement plans including bisque
jokes.punchline: Leslie's passionate anti-bisque stance
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Leslie's intensity about seemingly mundane topics

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Bisque? No. Absolutely not. Bisque is just a vehicle for cream and sadness. If I'm going to eat something that thick, it better be ice cream or regret.

dialogue.speaker: Stu
dialogue.line: It's a classic soup...

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: It's a lie wrapped in a lobster shell. You know what? Retirement is off. We're doing this forever.

jokes.text: Stew flutes for toasting
jokes.setup: Making a toast
jokes.punchline: Using tiny glasses filled with hot stew instead of proper drinks
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist visual combining formal toast tradition with hot soup

dialogue.speaker: Stu
dialogue.line: Using tiny glasses filled with hot stew instead of proper drinks

jokes.text: You let me pee with the door open so I could watch the end of Grey's Anatomy
jokes.setup: Listing signs of their relationship intimacy
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific overshare about bathroom habits and TV priorities
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor mixing intimacy with oversharing and TV addiction

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: We've reached that level of intimacy where we can talk about anything. Like, I know exactly what Leslie does in the bathroom, and she knows my TV schedule.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ann, you really didn't need to bring that up.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: No, it's sweet! Most couples don't get there. We're basically soulmates.

jokes.text: I'm just government junior
jokes.setup: Larry giving Ben advice about leadership
jokes.punchline: His humble self-description despite being a longtime employee
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's self-aware humility

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You know, Ben, I've been here a long time. And I've learned that the best leaders are humble. They don't brag about their accomplishments. They let their work speak for itself.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Me? I'm just a simple guy who pushes papers and tries not to mess things up too bad.

jokes.text: That was me. And I didn't really like it
jokes.setup: Larry boasting about his best prank ever
jokes.punchline: The victim's understated response revealing it wasn't appreciated
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan callback that deflates Larry's pride perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I pulled off the greatest prank in the history of pranks. I filled Jerry's office with balloons. Took me all night, but it was worth it.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: That's nice, Larry.

jokes.text: Who uses mousse still?
jokes.setup: Tom defending his hair product
jokes.punchline: Immediately questioning mousse's relevance
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment about Tom's vanity and product awareness

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: This mousse is incredible. It's got micro-nutrients and—wait, is mousse even still a thing? Do people use mousse anymore?

jokes.text: I don't know! Me either! Maybe let's not? Yeah, I think maybe not
jokes.setup: Chris proposes again
jokes.punchline: Both immediately change their minds in rapid succession
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of their indecisive pattern - rapid back-and-forth is well-paced.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Ann, will you marry me?

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Yes! Oh my God, yes!

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Wait, no, I changed my mind.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Oh thank God, me too.

jokes.text: Larry still can't see purple
jokes.setup: Discussion of Tom's past presentation failures
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of permanent injury from laser accident
jokes.explanation: Great throwaway line suggesting serious consequences from past comedy

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Remember when Tom presented that idea about the laser pointer system and it went into his eye?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: We don't talk about that.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: You lost vision in that eye for three weeks.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: It came back eventually. Mostly.

jokes.text: That was Larry's fault. He picked up the laser and stared right into it
jokes.setup: Defending his laser show presentation
jokes.punchline: Revealing Larry's absurd contribution to his own injury
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing both Tom's deflection and Larry's incompetence

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Look, I didn't push Larry down the stairs. He fell because he was trying to sneak into my laser show without paying. He tripped on his own feet while running away from security.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That's not true! I was invited!

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Larry, you were wearing a fake mustache and using the name 'Larry Gergich.' Your actual name is Larry Gergich.

jokes.text: Ben's elaborate fake kidnapping prank plan with divers and squibs
jokes.setup: Ben trying to prank his employees
jokes.punchline: His completely over-the-top Hollywood action movie scenario
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Ben's overthinking applied to pranks with absurd results

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Okay everyone, I have an announcement. There's been a terrorist attack at City Hall. We need to evacuate immediately.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: What? Where?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: The east wing. There's a bomb. We have ten minutes. Also, the terrorists have taken hostages and they're demanding one million dollars and a helicopter to the airport.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Oh my God!

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: And they've also poisoned the water supply. So don't drink anything. Also, there's a sniper on the roof. Everyone get down!

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Ben, what are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: And now the building is on fire. We have to repel down the side using the fire hose. Go, go, go!

jokes.text: What the fuck? Accidental squib explosion
jokes.setup: Ben's elaborate prank explanation
jokes.punchline: April's reaction to getting hit with fake blood effects
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy payoff to Ben's over-elaborate setup

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I'm going to kill you.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: It's just fake blood, April. It washes right out.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Not you. I'm going to kill whoever told you that you're funny.

jokes.text: Best prank ever
jokes.setup: Ben's prank going horribly wrong
jokes.punchline: April genuinely appreciating the chaos and violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing April's appreciation for chaos and dark humor

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: This is the best day of my life.

jokes.text: Just pick a thing
jokes.setup: Ben asking about her son's age
jokes.punchline: April's dismissive response to his fake emotional moment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing April cutting through Ben's attempted manipulation

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: He's three. And I know what you're doing. You're trying to have a fake emotional moment so I'll think you're sensitive. It won't work. I know you.

jokes.text: Just take it and get outta here
jokes.setup: Chris and Ann's multiple ring decisions
jokes.punchline: The jewelry store owner's exasperated surrender
jokes.explanation: Solid reaction comedy showing the impact of main characters' indecision on bystanders

dialogue.speaker: Jewelry Store Owner
dialogue.line: Look, I've shown you 47 rings. You've rejected every single one.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: What about that one?

dialogue.speaker: Jewelry Store Owner
dialogue.line: That's the same ring you said looked like 'a frozen tear.' Twice.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: What if we got two rings? One for each hand?

dialogue.speaker: Jewelry Store Owner
dialogue.line: You know what? Take them all. Take the store. I'm done.

jokes.text: Leslie's anti-stew presentation describing it as bent over boiling cauldron
jokes.setup: Leslie supposed to advocate for Stu
jokes.punchline: Her accidentally making stew work sound like medieval torture
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Leslie's self-sabotage through over-honesty

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Stu is a master craftsman who has dedicated his life to the ancient art of stew-making. He works in conditions that would make a medieval peasant weep—hunched over a boiling cauldron for hours, his hands blistered from stirring an endless pot of root vegetables and mystery meat. He endures the acrid smoke that fills his lungs, the scalding splashes that mark his skin, the unbearable heat that beats down on him like a torturer's hammer.

jokes.text: Pungent beef smoothies until we bury him under that shed
jokes.setup: Leslie's increasingly negative description of stew work
jokes.punchline: The morbid conclusion about Stu's fate
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark escalation combining Leslie's honesty with morbid imagery

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Stew is just a way of saying 'I gave up on seasoning,' and 'I gave up on presentation.' It's really just a way of saying 'I gave up.' And when you're eating stew, you're basically eating someone's broken dreams in a bowl.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: And honestly, I think stew is what they serve in hell. Like, that's the thing they make you eat for all eternity—just lukewarm stew with mystery meat floating around in it.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: So basically, if you're making stew, you're either very poor or you're dead. And probably, you're dead.

jokes.text: I think I've made a terrible mistake.
jokes.setup: Leslie's horrifying description of his future
jokes.punchline: Stu's understated realization about his career choice
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat - understated response to Leslie's over-the-top description

dialogue.speaker: Stu
dialogue.line: I think I've made a terrible mistake.

jokes.text: I think I've made a terrible mistake
jokes.setup: Leslie's completely destructive presentation
jokes.punchline: Her understated realization of what she just did
jokes.explanation: Good understated reaction to her own catastrophic self-sabotage

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I think that went really well.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, you just threw a shoe at the mayor.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Oh.

jokes.text: Hey. Rip it
jokes.setup: Tom's turn to present after Leslie's disaster
jokes.punchline: His casual delivery of his earlier catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Tom's confidence contrasted with Leslie's overthinking

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Treat yo'self.

jokes.text: R-e-s-p-e-g-c. According to Urethra Franklin, that's exactly how it's spelled
jokes.setup: Ben getting a respect song
jokes.punchline: Misspelling respect and mispronouncing Aretha Franklin
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay humor though relies on obvious mispronunciation for laughs

jokes.text: Then you betrayed me and turned them into efficient and caring government employees
jokes.setup: Ron explaining why he hired apathetic people
jokes.punchline: Framing good government work as a personal betrayal
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing Ron's libertarian worldview through the lens of personal betrayal

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I hire the least qualified people. I do this intentionally. Because if I hire someone who has a passion for government and public service, I can't fire them for not doing a good job. It would be a personal betrayal. But if I hire someone who doesn't care, and they don't do a good job, I can just fire them and feel nothing. It's basic management.

tmdb_overview: Leslie fights with Ben over questionable sales techniques at the local farmers market. Ann searches for a way to vent about the annoying aspects of pregnancy, while April supports Andy in a new creative venture.
insight: Understated ensemble episode coasts on character chemistry despite below-season-average joke density.
